methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $29.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Alpine Eagle is a seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Jan-Hendrik Boelens and Timo Breuer, having raised €10.25M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies, supplemented by a broader comp universe of ten private firms and two public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS).

The peer-median method, the applicable triangulation approach, identifies a median valuation of $300M across the five-company peer set. This cohort ranges from $60M (Tenna Systems at Seed stage) to $29.0B (Scale AI at Series A), with intermediate markers at $150M (Ares Industries), $300M (Vatn Systems), and $500M (Stark Defence). The wide range reflects stage and scope heterogeneity within Defense & Security, but the median anchor aligns with the clustering of later-stage peers around $150M–$500M.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $255M to $345M, centered on $300M. Key uncertainties include Alpine Eagle's specific product maturity, defensibility, and addressable market penetration relative to the peer set—factors that the valuation floor and ceiling attempt to bound but cannot fully resolve without proprietary operational data. The reliance on a single method reflects limited availability of directly comparable transaction data at the Seed stage in this sector.
